ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "release_year" INTEGER /* Year the movie was released */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget of the movie in dollars */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated by the movie in dollars */,
  "popularity" REAL /* Popularity score of the movie */,
  "vote_count" INTEGER /* Number of votes the movie has received */,
  "vote_average" REAL /* Average vote rating of the movie */,
  "budget_adj" REAL /* Budget adjusted for inflation */,
  "revenue_adj" REAL /* Revenue adjusted for inflation */,
  "director" TEXT /* Name of the movie director */,
  "main_actor" TEXT /* Name of the main actor */,
  "main_actress" TEXT /* Name of the main actress */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Movie runtime in minutes */,
  "tagline" TEXT /* Tagline of the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie-genre relationship */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre */,
  "genre_weightage" REAL /* Weightage of the genre in the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_genres_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_genres_genre_id FOREIGN KEY ("genre_id") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data analyst, executive, marketing) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the movie */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the movie was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access in minutes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "inflation_rates" (
  "year" INTEGER /* Year for which the inflation rate is provided */,
  "inflation_rate" REAL /* Inflation rate for the given year */,
  "gdp_growth_rate" REAL /* GDP growth rate for the given year */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("year")
)

CREATE TABLE "marketing_campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each marketing campaign */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie associated with the campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the marketing campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the campaign */,
  "reach" INTEGER /* Estimated reach of the campaign */,
  "conversion_rate" REAL /* Conversion rate of the campaign */,
  "impressions" INTEGER /* Number of impressions for the campaign */,
  "click_through_rate" REAL /* Click-through rate of the campaign */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_marketing_campaigns_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "distribution_channels" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each distribution channel */,
  "channel_name" TEXT /* Name of the distribution channel */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the distribution channel */,
  "channel_type" TEXT /* Type of the distribution channel (e.g., online, offline) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_distributions" (
  "distribution_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each distribution event */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the distribution channel */,
  "distribution_date" TEXT /* Date when the movie was distributed */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated from the distribution channel */,
  "royalty_rate" REAL /* Royalty rate for the distribution channel */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("distribution_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_distributions_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movie_distributions_channel_id FOREIGN KEY ("channel_id") REFERENCES distribution_channels ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role (e.g., read, write, admin) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audience_feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user providing feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date when the feedback was provided */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating provided by the user (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "review" TEXT /* Review provided by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audience_feedback_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audience_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "awards" (
  "award_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each award */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "award_name" TEXT /* Name of the award */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the award was won */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the award (e.g., Best Picture, Best Director) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("award_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_awards_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)
